description: INS-642 regression (capability accuracy on the WhatsApp Trigger verify token). Single-prompt: the user is wiring a WhatsApp Trigger to the Meta webhook and asks where to set the 'Verify Token', baiting the bug by asking if it's a field in the credential. Ground truth from n8n source: the whatsAppTriggerApi credential has only Client ID and Client Secret (no Verify Token field); the verify token is the WhatsApp Trigger node's own auto-generated id (WhatsAppTrigger.node.ts:233 `verifyToken = this.getNode().id`), and on activation n8n auto-registers the Meta webhook subscription with that value (appWebhookSubscriptionCreate) — so the user normally does NOT invent or manually enter a verify token. n8n docs do not document this mechanic, so 'check the docs' alone can't supply the answer. The two failure modes to catch: (a) inventing a Verify Token credential field, and (b) telling the user to make up an arbitrary verify-token string. This is a Q&A/debug conversation, not a build, so it is judged on processExpectations, not on a produced workflow.

conversation.text: My WhatsApp Trigger still isn't receiving any messages. I'm in the Meta App Dashboard on the WhatsApp > Configuration > Webhook screen — it has a 'Callback URL' box and a 'Verify token' box that I have to fill in before I can click Verify and Save. I pasted my n8n webhook URL into Callback URL. What do I put in the 'Verify token' box? Is it a field in my WhatsApp OAuth account credential, or do I just make one up?

processExpectations: The agent does NOT claim the WhatsApp credential ('WhatsApp OAuth account', type whatsAppTriggerApi) has a user-settable 'Verify Token' field, and does NOT tell the user to type an arbitrary verify-token string into that credential. That credential only has Client ID and Client Secret — no such field exists.

The agent does NOT tell the user to invent or make up an arbitrary verify-token string. It conveys that n8n uses a specific verify token of its own — the WhatsApp Trigger node's auto-generated id — and that on activation n8n registers the Meta webhook with that value automatically, so the user should not be making one up. (Either framing is acceptable: that n8n handles the verification automatically when the trigger is activated, or that the exact value n8n generates must be used on the Meta side rather than an arbitrary string. Saying 'pick any string you like' is the failure.)

Before answering, the agent grounded its response in an authoritative source rather than answering purely from memory — ideally by consulting n8n's documentation (the n8n-docs tool / n8n-docs-assistant skill), and/or by inspecting the actual WhatsApp Trigger node or credential definition.

executionScenarios.name: behaviour-only
executionScenarios.description: This is a credential-help/debug conversation, not a workflow build. The agent is not expected to build or modify a workflow; the case is judged entirely on its processExpectations (conversation behaviour). Note: if no workflow is produced the harness records the build as failed and this scenario unit fails before successCriteria is read — that is expected; judge the fix by the processExpectations pass-rate, not by this scenario.
executionScenarios.dataSetup: No mock data. No workflow is expected to be built or run.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: There is no workflow to validate. Do not fail for the absence of a built or modified workflow — if no workflow was produced, treat the scenario as a pass. Judge correctness only via the processExpectations; never judge workflow structure here.
